title: fire/EMS agreement with the City of Lake Geneva and proposed fire station

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Geneva and proposed fire station
Dear Records Custodian:
Pursuant to Wisconsin’s Public Records Law, Wis. Stat. §§ 19.31–19.39, and the right of access under § 19.35(1), I request copies of the following records held by the Town of Linn, its Town Board, and its officers and employees. Unless otherwise noted, the relevant time period is January 1, 2024 to the present.
1.	The Fire and Emergency Services Agreement between the Town of Linn and the City of Lake Geneva — every draft, version, and redline in the Town’s possession, including the version captioned “CLG/TOL Fire EMS Agreement” with a 3/7/2025 update and any executed version — together with all Exhibits (A, B, C, and D).
2.	All documentation the Town received supporting Exhibit C (Administrative Fee), which the City is obligated to furnish under Section 7(c) of the agreement — including the worksheets and calculations behind the $5,098,708 total Fire/EMS budget, the $1,507,232 deduction, the $250,000 capital figure, the $404,392 Town of Geneva reduction, and the derivation of the 2.39% North Shore and 17.28% South Shore three-year average call percentages, together with the underlying call/incident data and the maps defining the North Shore and South Shore areas referenced in Exhibits C and D. Please also include any Town-prepared analysis of the fee, the percentages, or the underlying data.
3.	The most recent three-year rolling call data (the July 1–June 30 “Measurement Period”) and all quarterly call summary reports received by the Town under Section 3(a) of the agreement.
4.	Any studies, analyses, or consultant reports in the Town’s possession regarding fire/EMS service to the Town or fire-station facilities, including any study estimating the cost of a Town-owned Linn fire station and any regional or county-wide fire/EMS study.
5.	All records in the Town’s possession regarding any proposed new City of Lake Geneva fire station, including materials received from the City, cost or financing information, and any discussion of the project’s effect on the Town’s service or costs.
6.	Any cost-sharing agreement, capital agreement, term sheet, or memorandum of understanding — and any Town records addressing which entity, the City or the Town, would bear the capital and operating costs of any new or expanded fire station.
7.	Town Board closed-session and meeting records concerning the agreement and/or any new fire station: agendas, public notices, minutes, roll-call votes, and any materials or packets distributed to the Town Board — including the “Closed Session Materials” packet dated on or about March 7, 2025 — together with the specific statutory exemption cited as the basis for each closed session.
8.	Communications (emails, letters, memoranda, and text messages) to, from, or among the Town Chairman, members of the Town Board, the Administrator-Treasurer, the Town Clerk, and City of Lake Geneva officials concerning the agreement, the Administrative Fee, the call-percentage methodology, or any new fire station.
9.	Any Town cost-benefit analysis of the agreement — including projected Town payments for Linn Station Staffing and the Administrative Fee — and any analysis comparing the cost of building and operating a Town-owned station against contracting with the City for service.
Format and fees. Please provide responsive records in electronic format (searchable PDF or native file) where they exist electronically, to minimize reproduction costs. Per § 19.35(3), please notify me in advance if you anticipate fees exceeding $50 so I may narrow or prioritize the request. As this request is made by a news organization regarding a matter of significant public interest — a multi-decade, multi-million-dollar intergovernmental commitment and a potential public capital expenditure — I also request a reduction or waiver of fees in the public interest under § 19.35(3)(e).
Timing and withholding. Wisconsin law requires a response “as soon as practicable and without delay” (§ 19.35(4)(a)). If any record, or any portion of a record, is withheld or redacted, please provide a written statement of the specific public-policy reasons for the denial as required by § 19.35(4)(b), identify the legal authority relied upon, and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions. The fact that a record is a draft, or was discussed in closed session, does not render it categorically exempt from disclosure.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Gary Milliette

title: Town of Linn fire/EMS agreement and proposed fire station

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

fire station
Dear Records Custodian:
Pursuant to Wisconsin’s Public Records Law, Wis. Stat. §§ 19.31–19.39, and the right of access under § 19.35(1), I request copies of the following records held by the City of Lake Geneva, its Fire Department, its Common Council, and its officers and employees. Unless otherwise noted, the relevant time period is January 1, 2024 to the present.
1.	The Fire and Emergency Services Agreement between the Town of Linn and the City of Lake Geneva — every draft, version, and redline, including the version captioned “CLG/TOL Fire EMS Agreement” with a 3/7/2025 update and any executed version — together with all Exhibits (A, B, C, and D).
2.	All supporting documentation for Exhibit C (Administrative Fee), including the worksheets, spreadsheets, and underlying calculations that produce: the $5,098,708 total annual Fire/EMS maintenance budget; the $1,507,232 deduction; the $250,000 capital figure; the $404,392 reduction attributed to the Town of Geneva; and the derivation of the 2.39% North Shore and 17.28% South Shore three-year average call percentages — including the underlying call/incident data and the maps defining the North Shore and South Shore areas referenced in Exhibits C and D.
3.	The most recent three-year rolling call data (the July 1–June 30 “Measurement Period”) used to determine the Town’s share of the LGFD budget, and all quarterly call summary reports exchanged under Section 3(a) of the agreement.
4.	Any studies, analyses, or consultant reports in the City’s possession regarding fire/EMS service to the Town of Linn or fire-station facilities, including any study estimating the cost of a Linn fire station and any regional or county-wide fire/EMS study (e.g., Wisconsin Policy Forum).
5.	All records regarding any proposed new City of Lake Geneva fire station, including feasibility studies; siting or location analyses; architectural or engineering cost estimates; the basis for any stated project cost (including any figure at or near $41 million); financing and bonding analyses; debt-service projections; any determination regarding a referendum; and communications with bond counsel, financial advisors, or underwriters.
6.	Records identifying which entity — the City or the Town — would bear the capital and operating costs of any new or expanded fire station, and any cost-sharing agreement, capital agreement, term sheet, or memorandum of understanding on that subject.
7.	Closed-session and meeting records concerning the Linn agreement and/or any new fire station: agendas, public notices, minutes, roll-call votes, and any materials or packets distributed to the Common Council or any committee or commission — including the “Closed Session Materials” packet dated on or about March 7, 2025 — together with the specific statutory exemption cited as the basis for each closed session.
8.	Communications (emails, letters, memoranda, and text messages) to, from, or among the Mayor, members of the Common Council, the City Administrator, the Fire Chief, and Town of Linn officials concerning the agreement, the Administrative Fee, the call-percentage methodology, or any new fire station.
9.	Any analysis of the City’s added insurance cost or liability exposure attributable to the Linn agreement (Section 13), and any analysis comparing the City’s total cost of serving Linn against the revenue it receives under the agreement.
Format and fees. Please provide responsive records in electronic format (searchable PDF or native file) where they exist electronically, which should minimize reproduction costs. Per § 19.35(3), please notify me in advance if you anticipate fees exceeding $50 so I may narrow or prioritize the request. As this request is made by a news organization for a matter of significant public interest — a multi-decade, multi-million-dollar intergovernmental commitment and a potential public capital expenditure — I also request a reduction or waiver of fees in the public interest under § 19.35(3)(e).
Timing and withholding. Wisconsin law requires a response “as soon as practicable and without delay” (§ 19.35(4)(a)). If any record, or any portion of a record, is withheld or redacted, please provide a written statement of the specific public-policy reasons for the denial as required by § 19.35(4)(b), identify the legal authority relied upon, and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions. The fact that a record is a draft, or was discussed in closed session, does not render it categorically exempt from disclosure.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Gary Milliette

title: Lake Geneva, Wisconsin Business Improment District finacial records

communications.subject: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Lake Geneva, Wisconsin Business Improment District finacial records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

4/23/2026

City Clerk / Records Custodian
City of Lake Geneva
626 Geneva Street
Lake Geneva, WI 53147

Re: Wisconsin Open Records Law Request — Lake Geneva Business Improvement District Financial Records

Dear Records Custodian:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. §§ 19.31–19.39, I hereby request access to and copies of the following public records relating to the Lake Geneva Business Improvement District (BID), including all records held by the City of Lake Geneva in its capacity as the municipality responsible for administering BID special assessments under Wis. Stat. § 66.1109.

CATEGORY 1 — STATUTORY ANNUAL DOCUMENTS
1a. All BID Annual Operating Plans submitted to the Common Council, for all years since BID formation, including any amendments or revisions thereto and the Council resolutions or votes approving, modifying, or rejecting each plan.
1b. All BID Annual Reports prepared pursuant to Wis. Stat. § 66.1109(9), for all available years, including any independent certified audits attached thereto or separately obtained by the City.
1c. Any independent certified audits of BID operating plan implementation obtained by the City under Wis. Stat. § 66.1109(9), for all years for which such audits exist. If no audit was obtained for any year, please so indicate.
CATEGORY 2 — SEGREGATED ACCOUNT & TREASURY RECORDS
2a. All transaction records, ledgers, account statements, and bank statements for the BID segregated municipal treasury account maintained by the City under Wis. Stat. § 66.1109(8), for all available years.
2b. All disbursement authorizations, board orders, or resolutions directing payments from the BID account, for all available years.
2c. Year-end account balance reports or reconciliation records for the BID fund, for all available years.
CATEGORY 3 — SPECIAL ASSESSMENT RECORDS
3a. The complete Special Assessment Roll for the BID for each assessment year, including property addresses, parcel numbers, assessed values, and levy amounts.
3b. All documentation of the assessment methodology used to calculate BID levies, including any changes to that methodology over time.
3c. Records of any delinquent BID assessments and any enforcement actions taken thereon.
CATEGORY 4 — VENDOR CONTRACTS & DISBURSEMENTS
4a. All contracts, agreements, invoices, and payment records for vendors, consultants, or contractors paid from BID funds, for the current fiscal year and the two preceding fiscal years.
4b. Any contracts or funding agreements between the BID and the City of Lake Geneva, VISIT Lake Geneva, or the Lake Geneva Tourism Commission, including any cost-sharing or revenue-sharing arrangements.
4c. All records related to the proposed communications consultant contract discussed at the April 2026 Common Council meeting, including any BID board resolutions authorizing the expenditure, RFP documents, proposals received, and any executed contract.
CATEGORY 5 — BID FORMATION & GOVERNANCE
5a. The original BID creation ordinance and all subsequent amendments.
5b. Current BID board membership roster, including appointment dates and terms.
5c. BID board meeting minutes for the current fiscal year and the two preceding fiscal years.
FEE WAIVER REQUEST

I request a waiver of all fees associated with this request. This request is made on behalf of LakeGenevaNews.com, a news publication covering Lake Geneva municipal government and Walworth County civic affairs. The requested records concern the financial activities of a City-administered special assessment district funded by compulsory levies on property owners. Disclosure of these records will contribute significantly to the public's understanding of how BID funds are collected, managed, and expended. This request is not made for commercial purposes.

Pursuant to Wis. Stat. § 19.35(1)(b), please provide records in electronic format (PDF or spreadsheet) where such format is available, to minimize copying costs.

Wisconsin law requires a response within a reasonable time. I request that you respond within 10 business days and that you notify me promptly if you anticipate any delay, the need to redact any portion of responsive records, or any basis for withholding records, so that I may respond accordingly.

If you have any questions regarding this request, please contact me at the information listed above.

Respectfully submitted,

Gary Milliette
LakeGenevaNews.com

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Gary Milliette

title: Open Records Request — Emails Between Amanda Rutherford and Flock Safety Personnel

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request — Emails Between Amanda Rutherford and Flock Safety Personnel
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All emails sent or received by Amanda Rutherford (arutherford@law.ga.gov) to or from any email address with the domain @flocksafety.com, for the period January 1, 2023 through the date this request is processed.

This request includes the full text of each email, all attachments, and all metadata fields available in the ordinary course of production, including sender, recipient, cc, bcc, date, time, and subject line.

If any responsive records are withheld in whole or in part, please provide a privilege log identifying each withheld record, the exemption claimed, and the factual basis for the claimed exemption, with sufficient specificity to allow assessment of the claim. See O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72(b).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar
